overall.description: First-person perspective from Cyberpunk 2077 showing a character slowly sliding down a tiled shower wall. The scene features falling water, a metal control panel, and dim artificial lighting with a greenish-teal tint. The character's hands with black nail polish are visible pressing against the wall before exiting the frame as the descent continues toward the floor.

scene_state.functional_zone: tiled shower stall with wall-mounted controls

agents.ego_description: sliding down the tiled wall with hands pressed against the tiles, black nail polish visible on fingernails

entered.detail: white square ceramic tiles covering the shower walls with visible grout lines
dark vertical metal control panel with circular buttons and fixtures
water falling from above creating a shower effect

segments.scene_description: The segment opens with a first-person view inside a narrow tiled shower stall. The character is sliding slowly down the wall, hands visible against the white ceramic tiles. A dark metal control panel with circular fixtures is centered in the view, and water streams down from above. The lighting is dim and artificial with a cool greenish tint.

events.description: character's hands exit top of frame as descent continues below eye level
tiled shower floor becomes visible as camera reaches lower position

agents.ego_description: continuing to slide down the wall toward the floor, hands no longer visible in frame

entered.detail: wet tiled floor of the shower stall becoming visible at bottom of frame

segments.scene_description: The descent continues as the character slides further down the wall. The hands disappear from the top of the frame, and the metal control panel appears to rise upward, shifting from mid-frame to the upper portion. The tiled floor comes into view at the bottom as the camera approaches ground level, with water still falling through the scene.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous first-person descent down a tiled shower wall, transitioning from a centered view of the fixtures to a low-angle perspective of the floor. It begins with the camera sliding slowly against white ceramic tiles, where hands with black nail polish press against the surface beneath a stream of falling water. As the descent continues, the hands slip out of the frame and the dark metal control panel shifts upward toward the top of the view. The scene then opens at the bottom to reveal the tiled floor as the camera approaches ground level, all while bathed in dim, artificial lighting with a cool greenish tint.
